title: veronica spicata subsp. orchidea
intro: Veronica spicata subsp. orchidea, also known as Veronica orchidea or orchid verbena, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of iridoids and caffeic acid. The health benefits of Veronica spicata subsp. orchidea include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
